propositions: The bot writes a heartbeat timestamp file once per 15s poll; freshness under 15s is healthy and age over 90s means the lander is down.
A Docker HEALTHCHECK stats the local heartbeat file and marks the container unhealthy when age exceeds 90s (must not probe Gerrit to avoid restart flapping).
An emergency-stop sentinel file on the state volume pauses the bot from consuming new Autosubmit changes and persists across redeploys.
On SIGTERM the bot first writes recovery.json (wipChain snapshot) then drains a near-complete submit within a 120s stop_grace_period.
During drain the bot keeps heartbeating each tick so the healthcheck stays healthy and autoheal does not restart mid-drain; recovery.json written first keeps recovery safe even if it does.
On restart the bot reconciles recovery.json against live Gerrit and S3 markers per phase before selecting new work, guaranteeing no double-submit and no stranded stack.
